perturbation.original_text: WCC grants a limited, non-exclusive license to XLI to use the West Coast Customs name, image, likeness and signature, including specific West Coast Customs Trademarks (including West Coast Customs logo trademarks and approved West Coast Customs vehicle imagery and trademarks) (collectively the "Trademarks") during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site event advertising, only in connection with the PROMOTION, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: WCC grants a limited, non-exclusive authorization to XLI to use the West Coast Customs name, image, likeness and signature, including specific West Coast Customs Trademarks (including West Coast Customs logo trademarks and approved West Coast Customs vehicle imagery and trademarks) (collectively the "Trademarks") during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site event advertising, only in connection with the PROMOTION, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'license' to 'authorization' creates uncertainty. 'License' implies a formal, legally protected right with specific conditions. 'Authorization' is vague and could be interpreted as a simple permission, lacking the same legal weight. This in-text contradiction impacts the entire section where the legal strength of XLI's right to use the Trademarks is discussed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WCC grants a limited, non-exclusive authorization to XLI to use the West Coast Customs name, image, likeness and signature, including specific West Coast Customs Trademarks (including West Coast Customs logo trademarks and approved West Coast Customs vehicle imagery and trademarks) (collectively the "Trademarks") during the term of this Agreement in connection with national, regional or local print advertising, together with Internet, television, video and on-site event advertising, only in connection with the PROMOTION, subject to all of the terms and conditions hereof. This license shall terminate automatically upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, at which time XLI shall cease all use of the Trademarks. In further consideration of this Agreement, XLI shall not in any way disparage the Trademarks, nor any of WCC's parent, subsidiary, or affiliated companies' trademarks or its or their products. WCC shall provide XLI with all applicable logos and usage guidelines for the Trademarks. WCC shall have the right of prior written approval over all uses of the Trademarks by XLI. The parties understand and acknowledge the importance of protecting the goodwill associated with their respective trademarks. Consequently, XLI hereby assigns to WCC all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with XLI's use of WCC's trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of WCC.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term "license," which implies a formal, legally protected right. The changed text replaces "license" with "authorization," which is a weaker, less legally defined term. The contradicted text refers back to the 'authorization' but includes language typically associated with a 'license,' such as "This license shall terminate automatically...", "WCC shall provide XLI with all applicable logos and usage guidelines for the Trademarks", and "XLI hereby assigns to WCC all goodwill and all other rights developed in connection with XLI's use of WCC's trademarks which shall inure to the benefit of WCC.". The use of 'authorization' in the changed text undermines the legal standing of XLI's rights and responsibilities related to the Trademarks usage as defined in the contradicted text, creating an in-text contradiction because the authorization is being treated as a license.

perturbation.original_text: XLI will provide WCC "Light Sheets" at no cost for the exclusive use in the limited operations and production to support the PROMOTION activities. WCC will request "Light Sheets" for use in operations and projects for XLI consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: XLI will provide WCC "Light Sheets" at no cost for the exclusive use in the limited operations and production to support the PROMOTION activities. WCC will request "Light Sheets" for use in operations and projects for XLI evaluation.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'consideration' implies that XLI has an obligation to consider the request and potentially provide the Light Sheets. By changing it to 'evaluation,' it suggests that XLI will only assess the request without any commitment to fulfill it. This contradicts the initial statement that XLI will provide the light sheets at no cost. The contradiction arises between XLI's supposed obligation and the discretionary nature suggested by 'evaluation.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: XLI agrees to provide the following to WCC as part of this agreement: 1. Provide WCC with five million five hundred thousand (5,500,000) common stock shares of XLI Technologies, Inc. These shares will be issued within five (5) days of signing of this Agreement. The shares are fully earned and vested upon signing of this Agreement. The shares also have piggyback registration rights. 2. XLI will provide WCC "Light Sheets" at no cost for the exclusive use in the limited operations and production to support the PROMOTION activities. WCC will request "Light Sheets" for use in operations and projects for XLI evaluation. XLI may provide "Light Sheets" at no cost for these uses at their sole discretion. WCC has no obligation to purchase "Light Sheets" if XLI is not providing them free of charge. If WCC elects to purchase "Light Sheets" from XLI, the cost charged to WCC will be the current wholesale distributor price.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that XLI would consider WCC's requests for 'Light Sheets', implying a potential obligation. The changed text replaces 'consideration' with 'evaluation', suggesting XLI will only assess the request without any commitment to fulfill it. This directly contradicts the second part of the contradicted text: 'XLI may provide "Light Sheets" at no cost for these uses at their sole discretion.' The change aligns the first statement about 'evaluation' in the Contradicted text with the second statment and now creates an in-text contradiction. Initially XLI agreed to provide the light sheets at no cost, but now that obligation is weakened to a discretionary provision, thus a clear conflict and contradiction arises.

perturbation.original_text: Any termination allowed by this Agreement will take place only after written notice of default has been given to the defaulting party, providing such party with thirty (30) days in which to cure the default.
perturbation.changed_text: Any termination permitted by this Agreement will take place only after written notice of default has been given to the defaulting party, providing such party with thirty (30) days in which to remedy the default.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'cure' with 'remedy' introduces a subtle yet significant difference. 'Cure' implies completely fixing the default, returning the situation to its original state. 'Remedy,' however, suggests addressing the problem without necessarily fully rectifying it. This contradiction creates ambiguity about the required level of correction needed to avoid termination. The conflicting interpretation impacts the termination clause and makes it unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination upon Breach. This Agreement may be terminated upon written notice by either party in the event of a default by the other party in the performance of any term or condition of this Agreement. Any termination permitted by this Agreement will take place only after written notice of default has been given to the defaulting party, providing such party with thirty (30) days in which to remedy the default.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'cure' with 'remedy,' which introduces ambiguity regarding the level of correction required to avoid termination. 'Cure' implies a complete fix, while 'remedy' suggests addressing the problem without necessarily fully rectifying it. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted section ('Termination upon Breach... providing such party with thirty (30) days in which to remedy the default'), impacting the termination clause and potentially making it unenforceable because the level of correction needed is ambiguous.
